name: Higgs inflation
summary: The Standard Model Higgs boson, with a large non-minimal coupling xi (h^dagger h) R to spacetime curvature, drives inflation at large field values. No new fields beyond the SM are required. The non-minimal coupling flattens the effective potential at high field values, giving a slow-roll plateau predicting n_s ~ 0.97 and r ~ 0.003.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces observed n_s and predicts small r similar to Starobinsky. Tree-level unitarity issues at scales below M_Pl/xi (Burgess-Patel-Trott 2009; Barbon-Espinosa 2009) and sensitivity to Higgs vacuum stability at high field values are open issues.

touches_benchmarks.note: Non-minimal coupling does not affect electroweak-scale Higgs phenomenology measurably; xi ~ 10^4 is irrelevant for collider observables.
r ~ 0.003 - 0.005 depending on reheating, in CMB-S4 / LiteBIRD reach.
n_s ~ 0.967 for N = 60 e-folds, in good agreement with Planck.

introduces.note: Single new dimensionless parameter xi; minimal in the sense that no new fields are added.

history: Bezrukov and Shaposhnikov (2008), 'The Standard Model Higgs boson as the inflaton', Phys. Lett. B 659, 703, arXiv:0710.3755. Critiques on tree-level unitarity (Burgess-Patel-Trott 2009; Barbon-Espinosa 2009) and Higgs vacuum stability (Bezrukov-Magnin-Shaposhnikov 2008) constrain viable parameter space. Critical-point variants (Bezrukov-Shaposhnikov 2014) lower the required xi.

references.citation: Bezrukov and Shaposhnikov, 'The Standard Model Higgs boson as the inflaton', Phys. Lett. B 659, 703 (2008)
